req.title: Fraud alert response (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-038 mandates three distinct test zones: (1) a valid in-window confirmation or denial while the alert is active (EP positive path covering the happy-flow state transition from ACTIVE to CLOSED/FROZEN); (2) a post-24-hour access attempt that must yield HTTP 410 (NEG/error pathway); (3) exact boundary behaviour at t=24h-1s (last valid second) and t=24h+0s (first expired second) using BVA. A fourth test covers the Decision Table axis: deny=fraud triggers card reissue. State Transition Coverage ties the ACTIVE→CLOSED and ACTIVE→FROZEN arcs together. All tests use fixtures for auth tokens and alert IDs, and time-travel helpers to control the clock without real waits.

tests.name: test_confirm_fraud_alert_within_window_closes_alert_and_reissues_card
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in state ACTIVE for the test customer
Alert was created less than 24 hours ago
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} using customer auth token
trace: R-FIN-038: alert closed when customer responds within 24 h
R-FIN-038: card reissued when fraud is confirmed
STC: ACTIVE -> CLOSED transition via confirm action

tests.name: test_access_expired_alert_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert exists that was created MORE than 24 hours ago
Alert was auto-frozen and transitioned to state EXPIRED by the system
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "deny"} against the expired alert ID
Assert response body contains error code indicating alert expiry
expected: Response body error.code is 'ALERT_EXPIRED' or equivalent
Account remains frozen (no state change from this call)
trace: R-FIN-038: error pathway – 410 returned when alert has expired
NEG: attempt to act on an already-expired resource
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_access_expired_alert_returns_410(
    expired_alert_fixture,  # <fixture>: yields {"alert_id": str, "customer_token": str} where alert is >24h old
    banking_api_base_url,   # <fixture>: yields base URL string
):
    alert_id = expired_alert_fixture["alert_id"]
    token = expired_alert_fixture["customer_token"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"response": "deny"}

    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body["error"].get("code") in ("ALERT_EXPIRED", "GONE", "410")

tests.name: test_alert_response_accepted_at_boundary_minus_one_second
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with created_at set to exactly NOW - 24h + 1s (one second before expiry)
Server clock is synchronized with test clock via time-travel helper
actions: Use time_travel_helper to set server relative time to T+86399s (24h - 1s after alert creation)
PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "deny"} at the boundary-minus-one moment
Assert HTTP 200 is returned (alert is still valid)
Assert alert.status == 'closed' and no reissue (deny path)
trace: R-FIN-038: boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested
BVA: last valid millisecond before 24-hour window closes (t = 86399s)
R-FIN-038: alert closed when customer responds (deny) within 24 h
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_alert_response_accepted_at_boundary_minus_one_second(
    alert_at_boundary_fixture,  # <fixture>: yields {"alert_id": str, "customer_token": str, "created_at": datetime}
    banking_api_base_url,       # <fixture>: yields base URL string
    time_travel_helper,         # <fixture>: context manager, sets server-side relative elapsed seconds
):
    alert_id = alert_at_boundary_fixture["alert_id"]
    token = alert_at_boundary_fixture["customer_token"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"response": "deny"}

    boundary_minus_one = 86399  # 24h - 1 second in seconds
    with time_travel_helper(elapsed_seconds=boundary_minus_one):
        resp = requests.patch(
            f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
            json=payload,
            headers=headers,
        )

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 at boundary-1s, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["alert"]["status"] == "closed"
    assert body.get("card", {}).get("status") != "reissue_requested"
    assert body.get("account", {}).get("frozen", False) is False

tests.name: test_alert_response_rejected_at_exact_24h_boundary
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with created_at set to exactly NOW - 24h (at the expiry instant)
Server clock is synchronized with test clock via time-travel helper
actions: Use time_travel_helper to set server relative time to T+86400s (exactly 24h after alert creation)
PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "deny"} at the exact expiry instant
Assert HTTP 410 is returned (alert is now expired)
expected: Response body contains ALERT_EXPIRED error code
trace: R-FIN-038: boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested
BVA: first invalid instant at exactly 24 hours (t = 86400s)
R-FIN-038: unacknowledged alerts auto-freeze; 410 on expired alert
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_alert_response_rejected_at_exact_24h_boundary(
    alert_at_boundary_fixture,  # <fixture>: yields {"alert_id": str, "customer_token": str, "created_at": datetime}
    banking_api_base_url,       # <fixture>: yields base URL string
    time_travel_helper,         # <fixture>: context manager, sets server-side relative elapsed seconds
):
    alert_id = alert_at_boundary_fixture["alert_id"]
    token = alert_at_boundary_fixture["customer_token"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"response": "deny"}

    exact_boundary = 86400  # exactly 24h in seconds
    with time_travel_helper(elapsed_seconds=exact_boundary):
        resp = requests.patch(
            f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
            json=payload,
            headers=headers,
        )

    assert resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410 at exact boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body["error"].get("code") in ("ALERT_EXPIRED", "GONE", "410")

tests.name: test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_without_card_reissue
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in state ACTIVE for the test customer
Alert was created less than 24 hours ago
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "deny"} using customer auth token
Assert card reissue is NOT triggered
trace: R-FIN-038: alert closed when customer responds within 24 h
R-FIN-038: card reissued only when fraud confirmed – deny must NOT trigger reissue
DT: decision table row – response=deny => closed alert, no reissue, no freeze
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_without_card_reissue(
    active_alert_fixture,   # <fixture>: yields {"alert_id": str, "customer_token": str}
    banking_api_base_url,   # <fixture>: yields base URL string
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["alert_id"]
    token = active_alert_fixture["customer_token"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"response": "deny"}

    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["alert"]["status"] == "closed"
    card_status = body.get("card", {}).get("status", "unchanged")
    assert card_status != "reissue_requested", "Card reissue must NOT trigger on deny"
    assert body.get("account", {}).get("frozen", False) is False
